proverb_native: Ligt gekomen, ligt gegaan
proverb_en: Lightly come, lightly gone.

proverb_en: He who follows the crowd has many companions.

proverb_en: No fish, shrimp is also good

proverb_en: He burns the candle at both ends.

proverb_en: One bee is as good as a handful of flies.

proverb_en: An estate inherited is the less valued.

proverb_en: When the wound is healed the pain is forgotten.

proverb_native: A quien Dios quiere bien, la perra le pare lechones
proverb_en: Whom God loves, his bitch litters pigs.

proverb_native: Con poco cervello si governa il mondo
proverb_en: The world is governed with little brains.

proverb_native: you pays your money and you takes your choice
proverb_en: you pays your money and you takes your choice

proverb_en: pack fights, pack gets along

proverb_en: Prepare a sleigh in the summer and a cart in the winter

proverb_native: Wer hat, der behalt’; Liebe wird alt, Unglück kommt bald
proverb_en: Who has, let him thereof take heed; love wanes, misfortune comes with speed.

proverb_en: Nothing bolder than the miller’s shirt, that every morning collars a thief.

proverb_en: He counts his chickens before they are hatched.

proverb_en: Let there be no lack of food in the pigeon-house, and the pigeons will come to it.

proverb_native: No hay placer que no enfade, y mas si cuesta de valde
proverb_en: There is no pleasure but palls, and the more so if it costs nothing

proverb_en: A golden hammer breaks an iron gate.

proverb_native: Man soll die Beute nicht vor dem Siege theilen
proverb_en: Don’t divide the spoil before the victory is won.

proverb_native: Una bugia ne tira dieci

proverb_en: All cats look gray in the dark

proverb_en: the best doctor at all times is one's own temperance

proverb_native: A homme hardi fortune tend la main
proverb_en: To a bold man fortune holds out her hand.

proverb_native: Gelegenheid maakt genegenheid
proverb_en: Opportunity makes desire.

proverb_native: Назвaлся груздем - полезaй в кузов
proverb_en: Called yourself a milk mushroom - get into the back

proverb_native: man muss die suppe auslöffeln, die man sich eingebrockt hat
proverb_en: you have to eat out the soup you made for yourself

proverb_native: Queres que te siga o caõ, da-lhe paõ

proverb_en: A little thing often helps.

proverb_native: L’homme n’a ni sens ni raison, qui jeune femme laisse au tison
proverb_en: The man has neither sense nor reason who leaves a young wife at home.

proverb_native: Fader og Moder ere gode, end er Gud bedre
proverb_en: Father and mother are kind, but God is kinder.

proverb_en: A little help does a great deal.

proverb_en: barking dogs don't bite!

proverb_en: No curse kills, no praise kills

proverb_en: “O what we must suffer for the sake of God’s church!” said the abbot, when the roast fowl burned his fingers.

proverb_native: Bem parece o ladraõ na forca
proverb_en: The thief becomes the gallows well.

proverb_en: Where the hedge is lowest, men leap over.

proverb_en: Love rules his kingdom without a sword.

proverb_native: De druiven zijn zuur, sprak de vos, maar hij kon er niet bij
proverb_en: The grapes are sour, said the fox, when he could not get at them.
